<findings>

[observation] [info] Cohesion of 0.75 with coupling of 0.25 is a healthy balance — the zone is internally connected without being overly dependent on the rest of the system.
[observation] [info] The scheduler pattern (usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts) cleanly separates lifecycle management from domain logic — this is a good architectural separation for background maintenance operations.
[observation] [info] The web-dashboard zone imports from this zone (3 imports) — ensure the public API surface of task-usage.ts is stable and minimal to avoid coupling the viewer to internal accumulation logic.
[pattern] [info] task-usage-analytics is the only zone in the web package with a provably acyclic, unidirectional dependency relationship with its consumer — the explicit facade module (task-usage.ts) is the mechanism that enforces this clean boundary.
[suggestion] [info] Document the naming convention split between 'task-usage-analytics' (zone) and 'task-usage.ts' (public facade) in a brief module comment in task-usage.ts explaining that the facade intentionally drops the 'analytics' qualifier to present a stable, minimal public name. Without this, contributors may rename the facade to match the zone name (task-usage-analytics.ts) and break existing imports.

</findings>

<insights>

- Cohesion of 0.75 with coupling of 0.25 reflects a focused, well-designed cluster with appropriate internal cohesion and limited external dependencies.
- The three production files cover distinct responsibilities — accumulation (incremental-task-usage), read/query (task-usage), and lifecycle management (usage-cleanup-scheduler) — suggesting good single-responsibility design.
- Tests are co-located proportionally: 2 test files for 3 production files, covering the most behaviorally complex services.
- Cohesion of 0.75 with coupling of 0.25 is a healthy balance — the zone is internally connected without being overly dependent on the rest of the system.
- The scheduler pattern (usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts) cleanly separates lifecycle management from domain logic — this is a good architectural separation for background maintenance operations.
- The web-dashboard zone imports from this zone (3 imports) — ensure the public API surface of task-usage.ts is stable and minimal to avoid coupling the viewer to internal accumulation logic.
- task-usage-analytics demonstrates a clean unidirectional dependency: web-dashboard imports from it but it does not import from web-dashboard — this is the only zone pair in the web package with a provably acyclic relationship, making it the healthiest inter-zone boundary.
- task-usage.ts explicitly documents itself as a 'public facade' in its module comment, confirming intentional API surface design — this facade pattern is not replicated in other zones and could serve as a template for reducing mcp-route-layer's coupling.
- task-usage-analytics is the only zone in the web package with a provably acyclic, unidirectional dependency relationship with its consumer — the explicit facade module (task-usage.ts) is the mechanism that enforces this clean boundary.
- The zone name 'task-usage-analytics' includes 'analytics' but its public facade is named task-usage.ts (without 'analytics') — consumers import from the facade and never see the 'analytics' qualifier, creating a naming split between the zone's internal identity and its external API surface. If the facade were ever extracted into its own package, the mismatch would cause confusion about what 'analytics' refers to.
- The three service files follow a consistent compound-noun naming pattern (incremental-task-usage.ts, usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts) but task-usage.ts breaks this convention by using only two words without a verb modifier — the asymmetry is intentional (facade vs implementation) but undocumented, making it an implicit convention rather than an explicit one.
- Document the naming convention split between 'task-usage-analytics' (zone) and 'task-usage.ts' (public facade) in a brief module comment in task-usage.ts explaining that the facade intentionally drops the 'analytics' qualifier to present a stable, minimal public name. Without this, contributors may rename the facade to match the zone name (task-usage-analytics.ts) and break existing imports.
- [call graph] 100 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 1 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
